---
title: "Video Demos"
description: "Short, reproducible video demonstrations for np and npRmpi, with captions, transcripts, and exact R scripts."
keywords:
  - video demos
  - RStudio
  - np
  - npRmpi
  - reproducible examples
---

These short demonstrations show the finished result quickly, then reveal the
few essential R statements needed to reproduce it. Every entry includes
captions, a transcript, the exact installed-build script, and direct download
links. Longer installation and platform instructions remain on
[Install and Get Started](R_RStudio.qmd).

## Onboarding and first results

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #first-np-result}
## Your first `np` result — 24 seconds

**Function:** `npreg()`<br>
**Takeaway:** one call selects the smoothing parameter and fits the relationship.

R is the computational environment; RStudio is the interface used here to
write and run the analysis. This walkthrough moves from loading `np` to a
fitted curve using the package's bundled wage data.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 24-second demonstration of loading np in RStudio, fitting an npreg model to bundled wage data, reading the selected bandwidth, and viewing the fitted curve.">
  <source src="www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result_transcript.txt)

Install the public package once:

```r
install.packages("np")
```

Then run the exact demonstrated analysis:

```r
{{< include www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result.R).

The example uses public CRAN `np` 0.70-5 and the 205 observations in the bundled
`cps71` data. No seed is needed because the example neither simulates nor
resamples data. Least-squares cross-validation selects an age bandwidth of
`1.892158` in the verified installed build.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/first_np_result/first_np_result_transcript.txt >}}

Once this works, continue to [Data Preparation](data_preparation.qmd) to learn
how continuous, unordered, and ordered variables should be represented before
using your own data.
::::

## Analytical demonstrations

The Italy sequence uses the same public dataset to connect conditional
density, conditional distribution, and direct conditional quantiles without
turning any one short into a full theoretical treatment. See
[How the four targets connect](density_distribution_quantiles.qmd#how-the-four-targets-connect)
for the population definitions and the important target-specific smoothing
distinction.

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #italy-conditional-density}
## Italy's changing conditional density — 25 seconds

**Function:** `npcdens()`<br>
**Takeaway:** the entire conditional density can change shape, not merely its
average.

The bundled `Italy` data follow per-capita GDP in 21 regions over 48 observed
years from 1951 through 1998. `year` is already an ordered factor, so the
estimator respects its ordered discrete support. The estimated density is
mainly unimodal after the war and develops two clear peaks in later years,
corresponding descriptively to the wealthier North and less wealthy South.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 25-second demonstration in RStudio of estimating and plotting the conditional density of Italian regional per-capita GDP over ordered years with npcdens.">
  <source src="www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density_transcript.txt)

```r
{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density.R).

The verified public CRAN `np` 0.70-5 build selected an ordered-year smoothing
parameter of `0.613508` and a GDP bandwidth of `0.570879`. No seed is needed.
These values are selected for the conditional-density target.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_density/italy_conditional_density_transcript.txt >}}
::::

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #italy-conditional-distribution}
## Italy's conditional distribution — 25 seconds

**Function:** `npcdist()`<br>
**Takeaway:** for each ordered year and GDP value, the surface estimates the
share of regions at or below that value.

Integrating a conditional density gives the conditional distribution at the
population level. This episode estimates the conditional distribution
directly. It does not obtain the fitted surface by numerically integrating the
preceding video's density estimate.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 25-second demonstration in RStudio of directly estimating and plotting the conditional distribution of Italian regional per-capita GDP over ordered years with npcdist.">
  <source src="www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution_transcript.txt)

```r
{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution.R).

The verified public build selected an ordered-year smoothing parameter of
`0.687927` and a GDP bandwidth of `0.307366` for the
conditional-distribution target. Five fresh installed runs were exactly
identical for the selected bandwidth object and fitted result. No seed is
needed.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_distribution/italy_conditional_distribution_transcript.txt >}}
::::

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #italy-conditional-quantiles}
## Direct conditional quantiles — 27 seconds

**Function:** `npqreg()`<br>
**Takeaway:** choose probabilities and invert the fitted conditional
distribution to obtain the corresponding conditional quantiles.

This example requests the 25th, 50th, and 75th conditional quantiles of
regional GDP for each ordered year. The default plot overlays those curves on
the observed per-year distributions.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 27-second demonstration in RStudio of directly estimating and plotting the 25th, 50th, and 75th conditional quantiles of Italian regional GDP with npqreg.">
  <source src="www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles_transcript.txt)

```r
{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles.R).

`npqreg()` first computes bandwidths optimized for the
conditional-distribution target, estimates that distribution, and then inverts
it at the requested $\tau\in(0,1)$ values. With the same data and selection
settings, the verified bandwidth values match the preceding `npcdist()`
example. This is a distribution-based quantile route. `nplsqreg()` is a
separate, regression-based location-scale method. No seed is needed.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/italy_conditional_quantiles/italy_conditional_quantiles_transcript.txt >}}
::::

The next pair uses the built-in `faithful` data to compare two unconditional
bivariate targets and to demonstrate the interactive `rgl` renderer in
RStudio's Viewer.

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #old-faithful-joint-density}
## Old Faithful joint density — 27 seconds

**Function:** `npudens()`<br>
**Takeaway:** target-specific smoothing reveals two distinct eruption cycles
in a manipulable joint-density surface.

The `datasets::faithful` data contain 272 observations of Old Faithful eruption
duration in minutes and the waiting time to the next eruption. The two peaks
correspond to short eruptions followed by shorter waits and long eruptions
followed by longer waits.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 27-second RStudio demonstration of estimating the joint density of Old Faithful eruption duration and waiting time with npudens, then manipulating its two-peak rgl surface in the Viewer.">
  <source src="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density_transcript.txt)

```r
{{< include www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density.R).

The verified public CRAN `np` 0.70-5 build selected bandwidths of
`0.146981` for `eruptions` and `2.925689` for `waiting`, optimized for the
joint-density target. No seed is needed. With `renderer = "rgl"`, the fitted
surface opens in RStudio's Viewer, where it can be rotated, zoomed, and
resized.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/old_faithful_joint_density/old_faithful_joint_density_transcript.txt >}}
::::

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" #old-faithful-joint-distribution}
## Old Faithful joint distribution — 29 seconds

**Function:** `npudist()`<br>
**Takeaway:** the same data can be viewed cumulatively with smoothing selected
for the joint-distribution target.

At each eruption-duration and waiting-time pair, the fitted surface estimates
the probability of observing values at or below both coordinates. `npudist()`
estimates this joint distribution directly; it does not integrate the fitted
joint-density object from the preceding video.

```{=html}
<video class="gallery-video" controls preload="metadata"
  poster="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution_poster.png"
  aria-label="A 29-second RStudio demonstration of directly estimating the joint distribution of Old Faithful eruption duration and waiting time with npudist, then manipulating its cumulative rgl surface in the Viewer.">
  <source src="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  <track kind="captions" srclang="en" label="English" default
    src="www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.en.vtt">
  Your browser does not support embedded video. Use the direct MP4 link below.
</video>
```

[Open or download the MP4](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.mp4) ·
[Download the captions](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.en.vtt) ·
[Read the transcript](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution_transcript.txt)

```r
{{< include www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.R >}}
```

[Download the R script](www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution.R).

The verified public build selected bandwidths of `0.0576794` for `eruptions`
and `0.186285` for `waiting`, optimized for the joint-distribution target.
Five fresh installed runs were exactly identical for each estimator's selected
bandwidth object and fitted result. No seed is needed.

### Transcript

{{< include www/videos/old_faithful_joint_distribution/old_faithful_joint_distribution_transcript.txt >}}
::::

The next reviewed episode returns to regression with NOMAD joint selection of
polynomial degree and bandwidth, followed by bootstrap uncertainty for the
fitted curve and its derivatives. Testing and `npRmpi` session workflows will
follow one reviewed example at a time.
